headline: Target outperformance signals shift to more durable growth, says Jefferies
summary: Target Corp (NYSE:TGT) shares have been among the stronger performers in the retail sector so far this year, rising about 20% and outpacing many large-cap peers, reflecting improving underlying business trends, Jefferies analysts have highlighted. The firm reiterated its ‘Buy’ rating on the...

headline: Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson's Award-Winning PAPATUI Puts Daily Sunscreen On Every Guy's Radar
summary: Men are twice as likely as women to be affected by skin cancer, yet far less likely to wear sunscreen. Today, PAPATUI, the award-winning men's care brand founded by Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson, launches a Daily Moisturizer with SPF 30: a lightweight, mineral formula, free of chemical UV filters, developed for men.

headline: PepsiCo Cuts Snack Prices Up To 15% After Doritos Jump Nearly 50%
summary: Price reductions follow volume declines, retailer pressure, and lost shelf space as Frito-Lay struggles to regain demand.

headline: Why buying sectors can be a losing game for investors
summary: Piper Sandler chief investment strategist Michael Kantrowitz joins Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman to discuss which stocks Wall Street is flocking to amid the Iran war, and how the bull market could return post-conflict.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Target (TGT) After Its Strong One Year Share Price Run?
summary: Investors may be wondering whether Target's share price still offers value after a strong run, or if most of the easier gains are already behind it. Target shares last closed at US$122.21, with returns of 2.9% over 7 days, 1.2% over 30 days, 21.6% year to date and 35.4% over 1 year, compared with 3-year and 5-year share price declines of 19.4% and 31.1% respectively. Recent headlines have focused on Target's role in the broader US consumer retail sector and how shoppers are responding to...
